reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to remediate the finding: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File. The command recommended: chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt /etc/gshadow-. That sets permissions to 0000? Actually u-xwrs removes read, write, execute, setuid, setgid, sticky; same for group; o-xwrt removes read, write, execute, sticky. That leaves 0000.

We need to apply that.

But must follow workflow: PLAN first, then review_plan, then apply.

So produce a plan: check if file exists, check current perms, then run chmod.

We need to read file? It's a binary file. But read_file just for inspection. Maybe we can check via stat. Can run "stat -c %a /etc/gshadow-". That's a command.

Plan: verify file exists with [ -e /etc/gshadow- ], then run chmod 0000 (or the specific chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt). Use run_cmd.

Will produce plan description. Then call review_plan.

We have approval. Need to execute the plan: check existence, check current permissions, apply chmod. Must execute one command at a time.

First run test -e /etc/gshadow- to see if exists. Use run_cmd.
We need to set permissions to 0000. Use chmod numeric. Use a command.
The user basically acknowledges the remediation succeeded. Likely they expect a response confirming. No further actions.
